chain.thought:
Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about the Chicago Bears draftee who attended the same college as fellow draftee Dustin Lyman. We need to know the college of each draftee. We extract the value from column "college" and create a different column "college of draftee" for each row. The datatype is String.

Select relevant rows.
The question aims to find out which player drafted by the Chicago Bears in 2000 attended the same college as Dustin Lyman (Wake Forest). We need to look at the relevant rows in the table that describe the players and their colleges, specifically focusing on those who attended Wake Forest.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:

attended the same college as fellow draftee dustin lyman -> college
column value of the question link to columns:

semantic sentence of the question link to columns:

The question is inquiring about another Chicago Bears draftee in 2000 who attended the same college as Dustin Lyman. To determine this, we need to group by the "college" column to see which other players share the same college as Dustin Lyman. Since both rows show "Wake Forest" as the college, we can compare the entries under the "name" column to find the answer. Using the f_group_column(college) operation will help identify players from the same college.
The Chicago Bears drafted Dustin Lyman and Reggie Austin in 2000, both of whom attended Wake Forest. Therefore, Reggie Austin is the other draftee who attended the same college as Dustin Lyman.

From the original table, we can see that Dustin Lyman attended Wake Forest. Looking through the other draftees, Reggie Austin also attended Wake Forest.
